summary: A Go-based testing and migration tool that translates Goss tests, runs assertions, and exposes functionality via MCP handlers, heavily utilizing AI-agent driven development.
concerns: Scattered duplicate directories (e.g., internal/baseline and GOrchestra/.../internal/baseline) suggest agent-generated code conflicts or incomplete merges
Heavy reliance on automated agent commits (glm-agents) may introduce inconsistent code quality
Metadata churn in commits indicates possible tooling overhead
suggestions: Consolidate duplicate internal packages generated by different agents
Establish strict merge guidelines for AI-generated code to prevent directory sprawl
Focus testing efforts on the core runner and MCP handlers
Review and clean up GOrchestra artifact directories
